Name: Royal Botanic Garden Edinburgh
Description:
We are a leading botanic garden and a global centre for biodiversity science, horticulture and education. We conserve one of the world’s richest botanical collections at our four gardens Edinburgh, Benmore, Dawyck and Logan.

Life on Earth is in jeopardy as we face an environmental crisis with catastrophic biodiversity loss and a climate emergency. These threats to our life support system demand urgent and collective action. Our response is to explore, conserve and explain the world of plants, pushing the boundaries of our scientific knowledge to find and apply solutions.

We engage nationally and internationally, empowering individuals, communities and partners to protect our botanic world, helping us build a positive future for plants, people and the planet.

Name: The Zoo Scientist
Description:
"The Zoo Scientist" was created in September 2015, first as a Facebook page, under the name "International Zoo News". The idea was to share news and information on the role of modern zoos in conservation, research and education. As this page always tried to focus as much as possible on the scientific side of zoos, the name was changed upon the creation of the website and twitter account.

The Zoo Scientist is 100% not-for-profit and it is for everyone! Zoos, zoo professionals, students and scientists of all areas are welcome, and so are general public and wildlife/animal enthusiasts. We aim to provide you with reliable, useful and educational material. 

We want this page to turn into one of your main sources of information for world-wide Zoo Science.

Name: Longwood Gardens
Description: In 1906, industrialist Pierre du Pont (1870-1954) purchased a small farm near Kennett Square, PA, to save a collection of historic trees from being sold for lumber. Throughout his life, Mr. du Pont indulged his passion for gardening, turning his farm into a magnificent horticultural showplace. Today, Longwood Gardens is one of the great gardens of the world, encompassing 1,077 acres of gardens, woodlands, meadows, fountains, a 10,010-pipe Aeolian organ and a 4-acre grand Conservatory. Longwood continues the mission set forth by Mr. du Pont to bring joy and inspiration to everyone through the beauty of nature, conservation, and learning. Longwood Gardens offers a breadth of mission programming that includes exhibitions, musical performances by top artists, renowned horticulture education programs, horticulture research, environmental stewardship and cultural and community engagement.

Name: Chicago Botanic Garden
Description: The Chicago Botanic Garden is a world-renowned living museum and so much more. Its roots grow throughout greater Chicago and beyond, inspiring all people to connect with plants. The Garden helps build healthier communities through urban agriculture, offers lifelong learning opportunities, leads pioneering plant conservation research, and showcases the beauty of horticulture. The Garden is a nonprofit organization run as a public/private partnership with the Forest Preserves of Cook County.

Name: Reserva Paulista
Description: Reserva Paulista é responsável pela gestão do Zoo São Paulo, Zoo Safári e Jardim Botânico.

Name: Aquarium of the Pacific
Description:
The Aquarium of the Pacific is Southern California’s largest Aquarium. It displays about 12,000 animals and more than 100 exhibits that celebrate the planet’s largest and most diverse body of water, the Pacific Ocean. Its galleries represent the frigid waters of the Northern Pacific, the temperate Southern California region, and the warm coral reefs and lagoons of the Tropical Pacific.

Each year approximately 1.7 million people visit the Aquarium, making it consistently one of the leading attractions in the Los Angeles region. Beyond its world-class animal exhibits, the Aquarium offers educational programs for people of all ages from hands-on activities to lectures by leading scientists. Through these programs and a variety of multimedia experiences, the Aquarium provides opportunities to delve deeper into ocean science and learn more about our planet.

Name: Loro Parque
Description:
En 1972, Wolfgang Kiessling fundó Loro Parque en Puerto de la Cruz, un espacio que comenzó como una reserva de aves exóticas y fue creciendo hasta convertirse en la mayor reserva de especies y subespecies de papagayos del mundo. Actualmente se ha posicionado como el mejor zoológico del mundo. Casi cinco décadas más tarde, nace Loro Parque Fundación, una organización con más de 22,8 millones de dólares invertidos en proyectos de conservación, educación e investigación a nivel mundial.
El Grupo comienza a expandirse en el mundo turístico con la adquisición del Hotel Botánico & The Oriental Spa Garden, en 1995. Uno de los hoteles de lujo más reconocidos en Canarias, parte del grupo Leading Hotels, con el mejor Spa de Europa.
Fue en 2008, que la compañía crea, bajo los estándares de calidad de Loro Parque y Hotel Botánico, Siam Park, el mejor parque acuático del mundo por séptimo año consecutivo, según TripAdvisor. Una experiencia de emociones que no deja indiferente a ningún visitante.
En 2015, el Grupo lanza Brunelli’s Steakhouse, el mejor steakhouse a este lado del Atlántico, con las mejor mejores vistas al océano. Apostando por posicionarse como número 1 en cada uno de los proyectos, Brunelli´s Steakhouse cuenta con el horno Southbend único en Canarias, que cocina a 800º consiguiendo una textura crujiente por fuera y manteniendo su jugosidad en el interior.
La última creación del Grupo fue en 2017, el acuario Poema del Mar en Gran Canaria, un tributo al agua a lo largo de 35 ecosistemas diferentes que muestran la belleza del gran azul en todo su recorrido, finalizando en el Deep Sea la ventana curva más grande del mundo, que sumerge a los visitantes en lo mas profundo del océano.

Name: Emirates Park Zoo and Resort
Description:
Emirates Park Zoo and Resort is a pioneer Modern zoo embraces the conservations, education and animal welfare while presenting unique unforgettable experiences to its guests. Our vision is “Better future for wildlife via a modern zoo pioneering the inspiration for wildlife preservation” 
Mission “Providing unique and unforgettable experiences embedded in education conservation to potentiate the visitors act to respect and conserve nature”
EPZR is a private entity consist of Zoo, Resort, Restaurants and Veterinary centers that provide wide variety of services and programs. 
EPZR is the first accredit Zoo in the Middle East that earned AZA (association of zoos and aquariums) accreditation “the gold standards”
Emirates Park Zoo has more than 1700 animals in its collection and has air conditioned area for summer time and outdoor areas for fine season. EPZR provides outstanding education programs being linked to the school curriculums and approved by MOE. Furthermore, it is a place of fun learning, interactive programs with ambassador animals, riding, animals presentations, shows and hands on activities including animal feeding and camping in the zoo. Ideally, it takes two days to experience everything at EPZR hence a very unique resort with different types of rooms is located in the middle of the zoo where the guest can stay and wake up with the beauty of nature.
Emirates Park Zoo, where the memories are made!

Name: Botanic Gardens of Sydney
Description:
We are a purpose-driven organisation committed to providing solutions to critical environmental challenges facing all life on earth and improving the quality of people’s lives through joyous connections to inspiring public spaces. Our vision is to see people, communities, institutions and industries
informed and inspired by power of plants and to support their conservation.

We are the stewards of three world-leading botanic gardens, Australia’s premier botanical research institute, and one of the country’s most loved urban parklands:
• Australian Institute of Botanical Science
• Blue Mountains Botanic Garden Mount Tomah
• Australian Botanic Garden Mount Annan
• Royal Botanic Garden Sydney
• The Domain

Botanic Gardens of Sydney is the registered business name of the Royal Botanic Gardens and Domain Trust, which is constituted under the Royal Botanic Gardens and Domain Trust Act 1980 (NSW).

Name: Bristol Zoological Society
Description: Bristol Zoological Society is a conservation and education charity which owns and operates Bristol Zoo Project. We lead ground-breaking conservation and research projects spanning five continents across the world. With nearly a quarter of all mammal species and a third of amphibians threatened with extinction, there’s an urgent need to safeguard our wildlife. From the forests of the Philippines to the shores of South Africa to right here in south west England, we work tirelessly with local communities to save wildlife, improve the environment and enrich lives.

Name: BIOPARC Valencia
Description:
Bioparc Valencia es un nuevo parque de animales diseñado bajo el concepto de zoo-inmersión, que sumerge al visitante en una cuidada recreación de hábitats naturales y le permite acercarse a los animales, vegetación y paisaje como nunca antes, sin barreras visuales. 

Un viaje de 100.000m2 donde el visitante se adentra en la extensa sabana, entre rebaños de antílopes, jirafas y rinocerontes, bajo la mirada de los leones. Se aventura en la espesura del bosque ecuatorial en busca de gorilas, rodeado de chimpancés y leopardos. Sigue la senda de los elefantes hasta la cueva más legendaria de África y se sumerge bajo el agua entre hipopótamos, cocodrilos y peces de colores. Descubre la exótica isla de Madagascar donde los lémures saltan sobre sus cabezas. Un parque zoológico que ya está considerado uno de los mejores del mundo.

--------------------------------------

Bioparc Valencia is a unique zoo. Its design employs the “zooimmersion” concept, in which visitors are surrounded by meticulous recreations of the natural habitats being presented. Social animals that live together in groups are displayed, as well as groups of different species that coexist in the same habitats. Predatory species are integrated into the visual space, but are separated from other species by barriers invisible to the eye of the visitor, ensuring the safety of both the animals and the people. The animals, vegetation and landscape provide opportunities for discovering the complexity of natural ecosystems.

Name: West Midlands Safari Park
Description:
West Midlands Safari Park is home to some of the world's most beautiful and critically endangered species, with a 4-mile Safari Drive, walk-through Discovery Trail, African Village and Lemur Woods exhibiting hundreds of exotic animal species with close encounters. The 200-acre site includes a wide range of catering and retail outlets and a large amusement area with over 25 rides and attractions providing the ideal venue for family days out.

West Midlands Safari Park is a leading business in the UK Leisure Industry providing over 500 jobs within a range of fields including catering, ride operation, retail, education, wildlife management, conservation, guest services, events, HR, Management, Health and Safety, Construction, Art, Design and Marketing.

There are three fantastic venues within the grounds, which host a variety of events including meetings, conferences, awards shows, banqueting dinners, weddings, public events and more!

Name: Phoenix Zoo
Description:
As a non-profit charitable organization the Phoenix Zoo provides experiences that inspire people and motivate them to care for the natural world.

The Zoo has served more than 42 million people since opening in 1962 and more than 1.4 million people visit annually. Located on 125 acres in Phoenix's Papago Park, with 40 acres currently developed, the Zoo is home to more than 1,400 animals including more than 30 different endangered or threatened species from around the world.

Name: PARC ANIMALIER ET BOTANIQUE DE BRANFERE
Description:
Le Parc de Branféré est un domaine dédié à la nature, où cohabitent 1500 animaux en semi-liberté dans un parc botanique multi-centenaire.

 Le Parcabout offre une structure ludique de 3500 m2 de filets dans les arbres, pour s'amuser en famille et passer une journée inoubliable en pleine nature.

 Nouveauté 2021 : création d'événements professionnels ( journée d'étude, séminaire résidentiel, team building, repas d'entreprises, arbre de noël, formation...)

 Situé à Le Guerno (Morbihan), à 30 min de Vannes, à 1h de Nantes et 1h15 de Rennes.

Name: Care for the Rare
Description:
Providing animals the highest standards of animal welfare through evidence-based, innovative habitat design and management planning.

As pioneers in the field, we understand that the design and construction of zoo, aquarium, sanctuary and wildlife park habitats plays a pivotal role in safeguarding the wellbeing of wild animals, and conserving species and have partnered with zoos, wildlife parks, sanctuaries and aquariums across the globe to deliver change. 

Gone are the days of generic designs that overlook the specific needs of each species as we revolutionise the habitat design process, tailoring facilities and management strategies to the fundamental needs of each species – bringing the functional complexity of the wild to zoos, aquariums, wildlife parks and sanctuaries. 

What sets Care for the Rare apart is our unmatched expertise in, and deep understanding of applied ethology and animal welfare sciences, coupled with decades of practical management experience. Our proven, evidence-based methodologies provide a solid foundation for creating habitats that not only prioritise animal welfare but also elevate the visitor experience and ensure the future-proofing of capital developments. 

By choosing Care for the Rare Ltd, you are embracing a new era of animal care and facility design decision-making, where every choice is backed by scientific rigour and driven by the goal of providing the best possible environment and management strategies for the animals under your care, enhancing visitor engagement and creating a profound connection between people and wildlife. 

Contact us today to witness the transformative power of our innovative habitat designs and management solutions, and together, we can create environments where animals can thrive, people can be inspired, and conservation programs can flourish.

Name: Parc Animalier de Sainte-Croix
Description:
Avec près de 360 000 visiteurs par an, le Parc Animalier de Sainte-Croix est devenu une référence en France et en Europe pour la présentation de la faune européenne et des espèces mondiales menacées 

Dans un écrin de verdure de 130 hectares, 4 sentiers de découverte emmènent les visiteurs à la rencontre de plus de 1 500 animaux issus de 100 espèces vivant en semi-liberté 

Pour prolonger sa visite, 52 Lodges Nature permettent aux amoureux de la nature de vivre une expérience unique : dormir au cœur d'un grand parc animalier au plus près des animaux sauvages.

Le parc fait parti du top 5 des parcs et zoos préférés des Français (TripAdvisor), du top 5 des parcs et zoos européens (Diamond ThemePark Awards 2020) et a été élu meilleur "zoo et parc animalier" de France 2 fois par le magazine Capital suite à une enquête nationale auprès de 20 000 consommateurs !

Name: Jardim Zoológico
Description:
Desde 1884, o Jardim Zoológico assume a missão de desenvolver e promover um parque zoológico e botânico de excelência, um centro de conservação de espécies vulneráveis e ameaçadas pela extinção. 
A investigação científica, os programas de enriquecimento ambiental, e a educação para a conservação, em conjunto, são determinantes para esta missão.

Name: Bioparc - Zoo de Doué-la-Fontaine
Description:
Au cœur du Val de Loire, le Bioparc est le seul zoo troglodytique au monde ! Plus de 2000 animaux y ont trouvé un cadre naturel exceptionnel.
Dans un véritable labyrinthe minéral et végétal, nos tunnels naturellement climatisés vous feront passer d’un monde à l’autre. Admirez les comportements des animaux dans leurs espaces XXL : ici pas de dressage, vive le spectacle de la nature !

Notre parc est récompensé par le public et par des partenaires réputés :
- Plan Qualité Tourisme : récompense ministérielle obtenue
- Tripadvisor : une note magnifique grâce aux avis de nos visiteurs
- Guide du Routard : l’incontournable guide nous classe « 3 Routards »
- Guide Michelin : le guide nous attribue 2 étoiles

Name: Odense Zoo
Description:
Danmarks hyggeligste Zoo! 
Masser af familieoplevelser og dyrevelfærd i top. 

Odense Zoo er den 9. mest besøgte attraktion i Danmark og den største kulturinstitution udenfor København. Årligt besøger lidt over 400.000 gæster Zoo.

Danmarks hyggeligste Zoo! 
Masser af familieoplevelser og dyrevelfærd i top. 

Odense Zoo er den 9. mest besøgte attraktion i Danmark og den største kulturinstitution udenfor København. Årligt besøger lidt over 400.000 gæster Zoo.

Name: Port Moresby Nature Park
Description: Spread over 30 acres of tranquil tropical gardens the park is home to over 350 native animals and hundreds of plant species, many of which are rarely seen outside of PNG. Port Moresby Nature Park is PNG's only internationally recognized Wildlife Park and sets the benchmark in PNG for tourism, animal welfare, conservation, and research and community nature-based education. It hosts over 130,000 visitors to the park each year. The Park has won a number of international awards for its work including the historic Zoo and Aquarium Association of Australasia (ZAA) award for its Sister Zoo Capacity Development Partnership Program in 2016, and again in 2018 it won an education award for small institutions for its World Wildlife Week program focused on educating school-aged children about the importance of native wildlife conservation and preservation. For its consistent delivery of its mission in wildlife conservation and research, the Port Moresby Nature Park has recently won two international awards in the 2021 Zoo and Aquarium Association of Australasia (ZAA) in the small institutions category for its Exhibit Design in the construction of the 'Reptile Haus', a first of its kind in PNG. In the second award, the Park again in the small institutions category won the Engagement Award for its 'Snaketastic' Program. Awarded the Trip Advisor Certificate of Excellence over 3 consecutive years it's the only attraction in Papua New Guinea to have done so.
Location: National Capital District, National Capital District

Name: Zoo Zürich
Description: Der innovative Zoo Zürich ist ein viel besuchter attraktiver Erlebnis-, Erholungs- und Bildungsraum. Als Naturschutzzentrum will er die Menschen für Tiere begeistern und sie motivieren, sich zusammen mit dem Zoo aktiv für den Natur- und Artenschutz einzusetzen. Denn «wer Tiere kennt, wird Tiere schützen».

Name: Faunia
Description:
Faunia, el parque temático de la naturaleza, es único por contar con diferentes ecosistemas y áreas temáticas en las que se encuentran más de 1.200 animales de 170 especies diferentes . 

En Faunia contamos con espacios exclusivos para eventos de todo tipo, desde bodas, bautizos, comuniones, eventos corporativos, team building, cenas privadas… Contamos con la tecnología audiovisual más avanzada y espacios de todo tipo. 

Llegar a Faunia es muy sencillo, solo te llevará 10 minutos en coche desde el centro de Madrid y poder aparcar en nuestro parking exclusivo para eventos, aunque también tiene muy buena combinación de transporte público.

Name: Fort Worth Botanic Garden
Description: Enjoy the captivating beauty of Fort Worth Botanic Garden (FWBG) and Botanical Research Institute of Texas (BRIT), one of the largest centers for botanical exploration and discovery in the United States. The new organization brings together BRIT’s fundraising, education, and world-class research capabilities with the Garden’s historically significant grounds, event facilities, and horticultural expertise. Together, FWBG will create one of the leading public gardens in America.

Name: Safari Thoiry
Description:
Aux portes de Paris et depuis 1968 le parc animalier de Thoiry offre à ses visiteurs des aventures uniques au cœur de la nature !
C'est une entreprise à taille humaine qui ne cesse d'évoluer et de se développer.

Name: San Diego Zoo Safari Park
Description: The San Diego Zoo Safari Park, originally named the San Diego Wild Animal Park until 2010, is an 1,800 acre zoo in the San Pasqual Valley area of San Diego, California, near Escondido. It is one of the largest tourist attractions in San Diego County.

Name: BIOPARC
Description:
Rain Forest S.L. es la empresa que gestiona BIOPARC Fuengirola, BIODOMO y BIOPARC Acuario de Gijón y BIOPARC Valencia.

Bioparc Fuengirola es el primer proyecto de zoo-inmersión de Europa. Este diseño sumerge al visitante en la selva y es quien tiene que buscar los puntos adecuados para ver a las más de 200 especies que se encuentran en BIOPARC Fuengirola. Recintos multiespecie, multivisión, sin barreras visuales y un diseño muy  cuidado simulando los entornos más amenazados y bellos del planeta hacen de este parque uno de los mejores de Europa. Se recrean zonas selváticas de África Ecuatorial, Sudeste Asiático, islas del Indo-Pacífico y la isla del Madagascar. 
“De nada sirve tener animales en cautividad si después no se destinan recursos a las zonas naturales de donde proceden”, son palabras de Jose Maldonado presidente de la compañía. BIOPARC trata de educar a los visitantes en la conservación de las especies y lo hace a través de la emoción de las experiencias vividas en BIOPARC. La emoción dentro de BIOPARC no se negocia, uno de los objetivos es conmover a los visitantes a través de la belleza de todos los recintos y de los animales que en ellos habitan. 
Por encima de BIOPARC, se encuentra la Fundación BIOPARC a través de la cual participamos en más de 25 proyectos de conservación alrededor del mundo. Esta fundación se nutre de los recursos que se destinan de los parques de la empresa.

En agosto de 2018, Rain Forest gana un concurso público para gestionar durante los próximos 20 años el Bioparc Acuario de Gijón. Una institución que se dedica a la conservación, investigación y divulgación de los fondos marinos.

Name: Dartmoor Zoological Society
Description:
In 2014, the Mee family kindly donated Dartmoor Zoological Park to ‘Dartmoor Zoological Society’, a charity specially created for the purpose and registered with the Charity Commission for England and Wales under registration number 1158422.

The charity is responsible for the care of the animal collection as well as our conservation, breeding and research projects.

The charity is completely reliant on ticket income, donations and purchases to keep the vital work going. Therefore, whilst we are still the beloved home of the ‘We Bought a Zoo’ story, a new story led by the charity is developing.

The animals here include tiger, lion, jaguar, lynx and serval, wolves, tapir, capybara, gelada, meerkats, monkeys, ostrich, a fantastic collection of bugs and reptiles and much, much more.

Our restaurant serves hot and cold, drinks and meals and we can cater for all kinds of events up to 200 people including weddings.

Name: North Carolina Zoo
Description: At the North Carolina Zoo, we celebrate nature. As the world's largest habitat zoo, we inspire a lifelong curiosity about animals for the hundreds of thousands of people who visit us each year. Our dedicated team of experts provides exceptional, compassionate care for the more than 1,700 animals that call our Zoo home. We also lead efforts locally and globally to protect wildlife and wild places because we believe nature's diversity is critical for our collective future. The North Carolina Zoo invites all of our guests to witness the majesty of the wild in the heart of North Carolina. and welcome everyone to join in our mission to protect nature's diversity. Visit NCZoo.org to begin your life-changing journey. An AZA-accredited facility.

Name: Palma Aquarium
Description: Palma Aquarium was founded in 2007 and is the property of Coral World International; the world leader in the creation of marine parks it faithfully recreates ocean and sea habitats and ecosystems with the aim of spreading awareness, knowledge and admiration for the beauty of the marine world and in order to encourage interaction with its many different species. It boasts a magnificent location on the island of Mallorca at just 50 metres from the sea and its 55 aquariums contain 5 million litres of salt water and more than 8000 different specimens of around 700 different species found in the Mediterranean Sea and the Indian, Atlantic and Pacific oceans.

Name: Aalborg Zoo
Description:
Aalborg Zoo blev indviet i 1935 og er kåret som Europas bedste Zoo i kategorien for zoologiske haver med op til 500.000 besøgende. Det overordnede formål er naturbevarelse, direkte gennem internationalt samarbejde omkring avl og naturbevaringsprojekter og indirekte gennem formidling af viden om truede dyr.

I Aalborg Zoo kan du holde møde, kursus eller konference med løvebrøl og isbjørne som bagtæppe. Vores moderne møde- og konferencelokaler ligger med byens bedste udsigt over Zoo i restaurant Skovbakken, der også har festlokaler til både firmaer og private arrangementer. 

Gennem vores partnerkoncept, Zoo Business, tilbyder vi vores samarbejdspartnere stor synlighed og co-branding med en af Europas ypperste zoologiske haver.

Vi arrangerer også brunch for grupper, børnefødselsdage samt overnatning for skoleklasser.

I Skoletjenesten underviser vi folkeskoleelever og studerende og afholder sommerskole for børn. Andre faste aktiviteter omfatter: Jul i Zoo, Dyrepasser for en dag samt den årlige naturvidenskabsfestival i efteråret. 

Aalborg Zoo er hjem for mere end 2000 dyr fordelt på 125 forskellige dyrearter.

Name: ZOOM Torino
Description:
ZOOM Torino è il primo bioparco immersivo italiano, una struttura unica sul territorio nazionale che fa vivere al visitatore il fascino e le emozioni della natura. Un viaggio appassionante attraverso l’Africa e l’Asia durante il quale vedere gli animali rappresentativi di queste zone davvero da vicino e in contesti naturali che riproducono le ambientazioni dei due continenti simbolo di biodiversità.

In un percorso pedonale che si snoda tra 160.000mq, ZOOM offre un viaggio evocativo attraverso 13 habitat in continenti simbolo di biodiversità gravemente a rischio, un percorso affascinante e suggestivo durante il quale conoscere le specie animali e i loro habitat attraverso l’esperienza diretta e l’educazione alla conservazione, grazie a biologi e veterinari che lavorano quotidianamente nel parco.

ZOOM ospita solo animali nati in altre strutture zoologiche EAZA (European Associations of Zoos and Acquaria, associazione della quale è membro) che vengono dati in affidamento al bioparco e che seguono programmi di riproduzione controllata allo scopo di preservare le specie a rischio estinzione, sostiene e promuove progetti di conservazione e collabora con le istituzioni scientifiche nell’ottica della ricerca e della sensibilizzazione del pubblico.

ZOOM Torino
Strada Piscina, 36 - 10040 Cumiana (To)
Telefono: 0456770033 – e-mail: info@zoomtorino.it
www.zoomtorino.it

Name: Meise Botanic Garden
Description:
Meise Botanic Garden is more than two centuries old and is a feast for
the senses. It is a place enchanting to the local hiker as well as to the
tourist looking for something more. You wander through 92 hectares of
nature, with 18,000 plant species from all over the world and with world-
class botanical and horticultural collections! You visit the castle, the
gigantic glasshouses, the gardens and you can learn all about wood In
the WOODlab. Different every day and every season at its best. Here,
science and nature meet each other in a picturesque historic domain. A
botanical pearl in the heart of Europe.

Name: Bloemenpark Appeltern
Description:
Appeltern Adventure Gardens (formerly De Tuinen van Appeltern) is Europe's largest garden inspiration park. The more than 200 sample gardens and the National Perennial Collection offer you garden inspiration and garden experience. The children have a great time on the Adventure Island and you enjoy nature, peace and quiet and breathe in the clean air of the land of Maas and Waal. The park is about 22 hectares and constantly developing. New gardens are constantly being created and new styles and trends introduced, so you never get bored and are constantly inspired. You can also gather lots of information in the garden advice centre, gain knowledge in the library, browse around our gift shop, shop at the plant market or have a sketch made in the sketch pavilion. Settle down on our terrace or enjoy a delicious lunch in one of our restaurants and your day out is complete. Your family or company party. team outing or event can be organised for you and is in good hands with us! We have several large accommodations, each decorated in their own style with all the necessary facilities to organise your event. And always located in the middle of green and inspiring surroundings.
Appeltern Adventure Gardens is open daily from 1 March until the last Sunday in November. For more information, current opening hours and calendar of activities, please visit our website: www.appeltern.nl. Contact: info@appeltern.nl

Name: Moody Gardens
Description:
Moody Gardens is a public, non-profit educational destination utilizing nature in the advancement of rehabilitation, conservation, recreation, and research.

Located on Galveston Island, Moody Gardens is home to the Rainforest and Aquarium Pyramids, Hotel, Spa & Convention Center, Golf Course, and more!

Visit https://www.moodygardens.com/employment to view and apply for available positions or email your resume to employment@moodygardens.org

Name: Poema del Mar
Description:
Situado en la ciudad de Las Palmas de Gran Canaria, Poema del mar es uno de los acuarios más vanguardistas de Europa. En él recreamos fielmente los ecosistemas de todos los mares y océanos del planeta.

Más allá del componente recreativo, Poema del mar nace con el firme compromiso de ser un referente en conservación de la biodiversidad y la excelencia en el turismo sostenible, promoviendo de forma activa el cuidado de nuestros mares y divulgando la importancia de este mensaje a nuestros visitantes.

Les invitamos a un acuario diferente, lleno de rincones maravillosos dónde descubrir especies que jamás habrían pensado que existieran. Ven a conocernos!
Location: Las Palmas de Gran Canaria, Las Palmas

Name: University of Minnesota Landscape Arboretum
Description:
Over 600,000 visitors explore the Minnesota Landscape Arboretum each year. The Arboretum features more than 1,200 acres of magnificent gardens, exhibits, sculpture, model landscapes, and natural areas - from woodlands,  wetlands to prairie. Throughout the year the Arboretum hosts world class exhibits, special events, conferences, tours and education programs... All designed with great care to inspire and delight visitors of all ages.
The fall and winter months are magical!  In addition to special events, winter lights and exhibits, visitors can explore miles of amazing winter trails.  Walk, jog, hike, snow shoe or cross country ski - the winter shapes, sounds and scenic vistas are breathtaking.  After, come indoors and explore the gift store, or art galleries. There is absolutely nothing like it in the Twin Cities!  
The Arboretum is an amazing place to visit as a guest - or become a member.  Either way you and friends can enjoy this amazing natural resource in every season.  What's more, the Arboretum is now accredited by the Alliance of Museums, reflecting our ongoing commitment to excellence and public service.
Experience the Arb today.  It's all here waiting for you to enjoy!

Name: North Carolina Botanical Garden
Description:
The North Carolina Botanical Garden (NCBG), part of the University of North Carolina, is a public garden focused on the display, study, and conservation of southeastern native plants. Through our educational, recreational, therapeutic horticulture, and research programs, we extend opportunities for connection with nature to people of all abilities and backgrounds. From the preservation of local biodiversity to sustainable gardening and event management practices, NCBG incorporates a conservation ethic into all parts of our operations.

The Garden's primary office, the James & Delight Allen Education Center, is the first LEED Platinum certified state-owned building or public museum in North Carolina. The main display gardens around the Allen Education Center include an extensive southeastern native carnivorous plant collection and habitat gardens that mimic the natural plant communities of North Carolina. NCBG also manages Battle Park and Coker Arboretum on the UNC-Chapel Hill campus, the Piedmont Nature Trail system behind the display gardens, Mason Farm Biological Reserve, Parker Preserve, and the UNC Herbarium, a collection of over 800,000 plant specimens. 

The North Carolina Botanical Garden is free and open to the public from 9 a.m.-5 p.m. Tuesdays through Sundays; the Garden is closed on Mondays and University holidays.

See current job postings at go.unc.edu/NCBGjobs.

Name: Jimmy's Farm & Wildlife Park
Description:
Suffolk Employer of the Year 2023

Jimmy’s Farm & Wildlife Park is an award winning wildlife park, rare breeds farm, restaurant and wedding venue based in the heart of Suffolk.
The wildlife park, owned by TV presenter and conservationist Jimmy Doherty, is home to world class facilities such as Europe’s largest polar bear reserve opened in 2023.

‘Sharing the Good Life’ encapsulates their belief that the natural world should be accessible and available to all and they have a newly launched charity called Space for the Wild which will drive their conservation efforts.

From pigs to polar bears the park is a centre of excellence for British rare breeds as well as camels, primates, tapir and crocodiles amongst other animals.

Name: Paignton Zoo
Description:
Founded in 1923, Paignton Zoo is dedicated to protecting our global wildlife heritage and inspiring in people a respect for animals, plants, and the environment.

With over 2,000 animals across 80 acres, Paignton Zoo is one of the South West's most exciting visitor attractions. It’s a place to roam, discover and get in touch with your inner animal.

Name: Parco Natura Viva
Description:
Parco Natura Viva è oggi un moderno Parco Zoologico, importante centro di tutela per le specie minacciate, che ha saputo evolversi nel tempo per assumere un ruolo attivo nella conservazione della biodiversità.

STORIA

Il Parco nasce dalla brillante idea dell'architetto Alberto Avesani, il fondatore, che trasformò negli anni '60 parte della propria azienda agricola, esistente dal 1933, in un'area faunistica, raccogliendo animali della fauna locale per allevarli ed eventualmente mostrarli al pubblico. Da questa idea embrionale nacque l'occasione di accogliere temporaneamente anche gli animali di un circo e da qui il pensiero di creare un parco, aperto al pubblico, per ospitare animali sia esotici che autoctoni.

I NOSTRI VALORI

L'impegnativo ruolo che il Parco Natura Viva di Bussolengo (VR) si è assunto, quale centro tutela di specie animali minacciate d'estinzione, è tanto ambizioso quanto stimolante. Oggi le funzioni istituzionali di un moderno giardino zoologico abbracciano la conservazione della biodiversità sotto molteplici aspetti e si possono riassumere in tre fondamenti:

 Conservazione
 Ricerca
 Educazione ambientale

PARCO NATURA VIVA PER LE AZIENDE E IL B2B

Il Parco Natura Viva è un moderno giardino zoologico che offre spazi unici per meeting, convegni, attività motivazionali e team building, congressi, cene aziendali, ambient marketing, spazi espositivi, e molto altro ancora. 
Una location innovativa, sorprendente e alternativa, immersa nella natura che renderà unico il tuo evento aziendale e che permetterà di promuovere il tuo brand, grazie ad accordi B2B, sponsorizzazioni o eventi esclusivi, indoor e outdoor, organizzati in base alle tue richieste ed esigenze.

IL PARCO IN BREVE:

40 ettari di collina morenica e bosco di querce
1.500 animali di 200 specie dal mondo intero
1 sezione pedonale e 1 sezione safari auto
Apertura: marzo-gennaio
500.000 visitatori/anno

Name: Walter Zoo AG Gossau
Description:
Faszinieren, Bilden, Schützen. Der Walter Zoo in Gossau SG begeistert grosse und kleine Entdecker. 1'000 Tiere aus rund 100 verschiedenen Tierarten machen den Besuch zu einem einmaligen Erlebnis. Der Zoo ist 365 Tage im Jahr geöffnet. 

März bis Oktober von 09.00 bis 18.00 Uhr
November bis Februar 09.00 bis 17.00 Uhr


Events im Walter Zoo
Es gibt viele Möglichkeiten im Walter Zoo, um sein ganz persönliches Fest zu feiern oder eine spezielle Veranstaltung durchzuführen. Ob in einer grossen Gesellschaft, für Apéros oder Bankette, für einen Kindergeburtstag oder am Sonntagsbrunch. Und ein Highlight ist die Übernachtung im Zoo (Nachtschwärmer).

Events für Firmen oder Teams
Für Seminare, Team- oder Kundenanlässe bieten wir eine einzigartige Plattform. Mit einer Erlebnisführung, Tierbegegnung oder einem Tiervortrag kann der Event abgerundet werden.

Angebot für Schulen
Als Ziel für die Schulreise ist der Walter Zoo ist verschiedenen Gründen naheliegend. Schülerinnen und Schüler der Klassenstufen Kindergarten bis Sekundarstufe II erfahren zudem in der Zooschule in lehrreichen und interaktiven Themenführungen und Workshops Spannendes über unsere Umwelt und die faszinierende Welt der Tiere. Der ausserschulische Lernort in der Zooschule ist die perfekte Ergänzung zum Schulzimmer. Die Schülerinnen und Schüler erleben den gelernten Stoff hautnah und mit all ihren Sinnen.

Walter Zoo AG Gossau

Name: Botanic Gardens and State Herbarium, South Australia
Description:
The Botanic Gardens and State Herbarium (BGSH) comprises three beautiful public botanic gardens, Botanic Park, the State Herbarium and the South Australian Seed Conservation Centre. 

Our mission is to build an understanding and appreciation of the botanical world.

We manage living, cultural and preserved collections, and use our science and research to improve understanding of the botanical world.

Our science is vital to support our community’s resilience and sustainability, and it underpins evidence-based decisions that inform effective legislation, policies, education, management and land use practices.

We engage with the wider community to share stories about plants and enhance understanding and appreciation of the importance of plants in sustaining life, and as a key part of cultural identity. 

Our places provide unique experiences and learning opportunities for visitors, and create an urban oasis of peace and tranquillity.

BGSH is a state government statutory authority governed by the Department for Environment and Water, and the Board of the Botanic Gardens and State Herbarium.

Name: Espace Rambouillet
Description:
L’espace Rambouillet, parc forestier et animalier fête ses 50 ans. Créé par l’Office national des forêts sur une idée visionnaire : nous permettre de nous reconnecter à la nature et mieux la comprendre en vous immergeant dans la forêt mystérieuse et légendaire. 

Pour cette saison anniversaire - et les 30 prochaines années – la société à mission Rambouillet Nature va prendre soin de la forêt et de ses hôtes. 
Visiter l’Espace Rambouillet c’est partir à la rencontre des grands animaux de la forêt, cerfs, chevreuils, daims, sangliers et comprendre leur écosystème. 

Visiter l’Espace Rambouillet c’est aussi s’envoler avec les aigles, hiboux et autres faucons et comprendre leur rôle dans la chaîne de la vie. 
La forêt des cerfs, parcours familial permet de découvrir les secrets du noble animal tout en vous amusant. 

S’amuser et apprendre sera le fil rouge de votre visite : visite guidée en train forestier, carnet d’exploration, sentiers thématiques et événements saisonniers sont au programme. 

Et puisque le grand air donne faim nous avons imaginé une offre de restauration pour toute la famille.

Name: Naples Zoo at Caribbean Gardens
Description:
Naples Zoo at Caribbean Gardens is a nonprofit charitable organization caring for rare species both in and outside the wild. Wildlife from anteaters to zebras live within this historic botanical garden founded in 1919 where guests can wander the paths, learn from animal care staff, and embark on a guided cruise through islands of primates. Its conservation and education mission focuses on programs that create a better future for both people and wildlife. With the support of visitors, members, and donors, Naples Zoo has invested over 2 million dollars saving plants and animals in the wild since 2014. See our current field conservation programs at http://www.napleszoo.org/conserve.  
 
Naples Zoo is accredited by the Association of Zoos and Aquariums, the international organization that sets the highest standards for wildlife institutions. Learn more how you can join in supporting Naples Zoo to create a better future for all living things.

Tierpark Nadermann

Genießen Sie Ihren Tierparkspaziergang und nehmen Sie sich Zeit für viele interessante tierische Entdeckungen! Von exotischen Raubkatzen, über eine begehbare Affenanlage bis hin zu einem Streichelzoo, sind viele verschiedene Tierarten zu bestaunen. Besonders Kinder können sich auf unseren großen Abenteuer-Spielplätzen nach Herzenslust austoben. Die Gastronomie sorgt für das leibliche Wohl. Das Tierpark-Team freut sich TIERISCH auf Ihren Besuch!

Name: The Garden Club of Toronto
Description:
The Garden Club of Toronto is a Canadian horticultural and floral design association whose members are active in environmental, floral and garden-related projects that benefit the community.

For over 75 years, the Garden Club of Toronto has played a key role in designing and planting gardens that beautify the city and environs as well as providing outreach and educational programming. True to its vision of sharing the wonder of nature through education, engagement and creativity, the GCT has contributed close to $9 million in current dollars to civic planting and its other charitable activities.

In 1958, the Club founded and provided ongoing funding for the Civic Garden Centre, known today as the Toronto Botanical Garden. Together with Landscape Ontario, the Club also co-founded Canada Blooms, the highly successful Toronto spring flower festival that showcases excellence in horticulture and design.

Name: Zoopark Zájezd
Description: Zoopark Zájezd is a beautiful family zoo just outside Prague towards Kladno. Over 100 species of exotic and domestic animals live here happily, including meerkats, many species of lemurs, and the largest public collection of chameleons in the world. Favourites include cheeky squirrels that might jump on your head, and other European and Czech species that the zoo protects in exhibits and in the wild. There's a contact zoo and a large playground for happy kids, and good food, brewery beer and roasted coffee for happy parents. It's a zoo for everyone. For beautiful experiences, relaxation and the joy of nature.

Description: Zoological Disaster Response, Rescue, and Recovery (ZDR3) manages and coordinates an emergency response network that fills a unique need in the exotic animal industry by facilitating the provision of experienced personnel to exotic animal facilities (eg, zoos, aquariums, sanctuaries) in times of natural disasters or catastrophic events.

* Guided Tours: Learn fascinating facts about butterfly behavior and the wonders of metamorphosis.
* Photography Opportunities: Capture stunning images of these delicate creatures in their natural habitat.
* Free Return Pass: Enjoy unlimited visits throughout your vacation.
* Daily Tours: Open 8:30 AM - 4:30 PM. The last guided tour of the day starts at 4pm.
* Cool Down at our Nectar Cafe: Enjoy refreshing lemonades or ice coffee.

Name: ZOOLOGICO GUADALAJARA
Description:
Zoo Guadalajara is a decentralized public agency of the Municipality of Guadalajara, governed by a Board of Directors. Located within the Barranca de Huentitán, it was founded with the vision of bringing people closer to nature and strengthening a long-term commitment to wildlife conservation.

With nearly four decades of service, Zoo Guadalajara is considered the only self-funded public zoo in Mexico. It spans 50 fully developed hectares with 5 additional hectares under expansion, respecting the site’s natural topography and existing vegetation. Today, it is a space dedicated to the protection of wildlife, home to more than 6,000 animals representing 300+ species, and welcoming over 1.3 million visitors each year.

We are a leading institution in Latin America, recognized for innovation, high standards, and a science-based approach to animal welfare, veterinary medicine, professional animal care, and conservation. We advance ex situ conservation and conservation education programs designed to inspire, connect, and educate our audiences. We are accredited by AZA and ALPZA and participate in EAZA ex situ conservation programs.

Name: Massimo Crippa |Progettazione giardini e agronomo
Description:
Benvenuto, sono Massimo Crippa, paesaggista e agronomo.

Aiuto i miei clienti a creare il loro giardino su misura, sostenibile nel tempo e in armonia con la natura.



Una profonda conoscenza delle specie arboree, l’interpretazione dei desideri del mio committente, una particolare attenzione alla sostenibilità ambientale e idrica e la profonda conoscenza delle tecniche di realizzazione sono le competenze acquisite nel corso degli anni che mi permettono di seguire i miei progetti dall’idea alla realizzazione.

La mia missione è realizzare giardini unici, capaci di ispirare e avvicinare le persone alla bellezza della natura, donando una sensazione di pace e benessere a chi li vivrà.

Personalizzazione e bellezza
Immaginate, sognate e poi mi raccontate …. e lo costruiremo. Il progetto parte dalle persone, negli anni ho imparato ad interpretare i sogni dei miei clienti, coniugarli con la natura per trasformarli in realtà.

Sostenibilità e biodiversità
Ogni pianta è scelta con cura, tenendo conto delle esigenze ambientali e della capacità di promuovere la biodiversità. Creo luoghi non solo belli e funzionali, ma anche vitali e sostenibili, dove l'uomo e la natura possono coesistere in perfetta armonia.

Equilibrio e armonia
Equilibrio e armonia tra le piante del giardino, il contesto e le persone che ne godranno. Questi sono i principi per creare ambienti accoglienti e suggestivi, dove la natura si integra armoniosamente con l'architettura e le persone.

Name: Wildwood Wildlife Park Zoo and Safari
Description: Wildwood Wildlife Park Zoo & Safari is home to over 1,700 animals and 229 species! A visit to the Giraffe Serengeti brings you close enough to one of the tallest mammals in the world. When the giraffe leans down to nibble out of your hand, they might steal a kiss from you instead! The Parakeet Encounter is on everyone’s favorite list! These colorful comical characters are sure to make everyone smile when they land on your hand, head or shoulder just to say “Hi”. Guests will have the opportunity to hand feed many of our animals “critter food.” This is zoo approved treats that can be purchased in the gift shop. Or you can purchase petting food pellets from our vending machines throughout the park. Don’t be surprised when many of our residents come looking for a hand-out! Critter food/ petting food pellets can be fed to goats, sheep, deer, camel and aoudads. Or buy trout pellets from the feed machines and toss into the pond and see the entire surface come alive with a frenzy of trout or koi. Guests can just sit and relax in this peaceful and tranquil area.

Name: Pro Gardening Blog
Description:
Pro Gardening Blog is a site for everyone who wants to start gardening or is already into it and wants more information. Whether you have a spacious garden or have limited space on your balcony to grow your favorite crops, this is your site.

Our mission is to help gardeners excel in health, knowledge, and enthusiasm. We help make community and home gardens more vibrant and productive.

So, we will guide you whether you are growing veggies, fruits, herbs, lawns, flowers, trees, or houseplants. We will give you the necessary information to grow and maintain thriving and sustainable food gardens and landscapes.

Some of the gardening topics we cover here in detail include:
Eco-gardening - here you will learn the best gardening methods that have a lesser negative impact on the planet. 
Landscape gardening - how to grow ornamental plants like shrubs, flowers, and trees
Edible gardens - this page will teach you how to grow your own food
Indoor gardening - learn how to grow your veggies or ornamental plants indoors
Plant problems - you will learn how to prevent, diagnose and treat plant problems like pests and diseases.
Creative gardening methods like hydroponics, aquaponics, and others
Gardening tricks and tips
Best gardening tools

Additionally, our team comprises professional gardeners from different corners of the world. These individuals have extensive experience in gardening, which is largely felt in the gardening guides we publish.

Last but not least, we are your friend; we are approachable and take feedback positively. And we are like you in many ways; we play with the soil, we talk to plants, we sit alone in the garden counting insects, and above all, we love to see our gardens thriving.

Name: Gairn Community Garden
Description:
Gairn Community Garden is a volunteer-led initiative in Aberdeen, Scotland, dedicated to transforming a once-abandoned allotment into a thriving, multi-functional green space. We aim to bring together local residents, community groups, and organizations to create a welcoming environment for learning, growing, and social interaction.

Our initiatives include:
	•	Educational Engagement: Partnering with nurseries and schools to teach children about gardening and sustainability.
	•	Social Well-being: Providing a space for older residents and the wider community to connect and combat social isolation.
	•	Play & Grow Areas: Creating child-friendly play spaces, raised beds for local food production, and interactive learning experiences.
	•	Community Collaboration: Working with local charities, businesses, and volunteers to enhance the space and support social inclusion.

We are always looking for volunteers, donations, and partnerships to help expand our impact. Join us in making Gairn Community Garden a vibrant and sustainable hub for all!

Name: Wildpark Peter und Paul St. Gallen
Description:
Der Wildpark Peter und Paul liegt auf dem Rosenberg nordöstlich von St. Gallen. Er gehört zu einem wichtigen Naherholungsgebiet der Stadt. Der Wildpark beherbergt verschiedene vorwiegend einheimische Tierarten, wie Steinböcke, Gämsen, Luchse, Hirsche, Wildschweine, Murmeltiere und Wildkatzen. Der Eintritt ist frei und rund um die Uhr und das ganze Jahr möglich. Der Wildpark besitzt ein Besucherzentrum und ist zu Fuss von mehreren Ausgangspunkten gut zu erreichen.

Der Wildpark wird von einer gemeinnützigen Wildparkgesellschaft betrieben, die sich zu zwei Dritteln aus privaten Spenden und Legaten finanziert. Das Wärterteam besteht aus 3 Personen mit 230 Stellenprozenten, die den Park und die Tiere pflegen.

Zum Park gehört ein Restaurant mit Terrasse.

Name: Blithewold Mansion, Gardens and Arboretum
Description:
Blithewold is a 33-acre summer estate with grand views of Narragansett Bay and is nationally significant in American history as one of the most fully developed and authentic examples of the Country Place Era. The property features a 45-room mansion filled with family heirlooms, and is framed by a series of lovely gardens that range in character from mysterious to exotic and from poetic to practical. An exceptional collection of rare and unusual plants, specimen trees, an accessible greenhouse, and whimsical stonework project a character that is romantic, fresh, and inspiring – and unique to Blithewold.

Our Mission is to preserve New England’s finest garden estate through excellence in horticulture and historic preservation, and by our example to teach and inspire others. Come and experience Blithewold today.

Name: Leintalzoo Schwaigern
Description:
Der privat geführte Leintalzoo ist ein kleiner Tierpark in einer naturnahen Parkanlage. Die Hauptattraktion ist eine der größten Schimpansengruppen Deutschlands mit über 30 Tieren, die eindrucksvolle Beobachtungen des Soziallebens unserer nächsten Verwandten ermöglicht. 

Affen, Vögel und Haustiere bilden einen Schwerpunkt, wobei viele der ca. 250 Tiere in rund 40 Arten ehemals ungewollte bzw. gerettete Tiere aus dem Tierschutz sind. 

Ein Naturlehrpfad, ein Schauteich mit Glasscheibe für den Blick unter Wasser sowie die naturnahe Parkgestaltung mit verwilderten Bereichen laden zum Kennenlernen der heimischen Natur ein. Junge Besucher erfreuen sich am Füttern von Meerschweinchen, Kaninchen, Schafen, Ziegen und Ponys. Ein großer schattiger Spielplatz neben dem Bistro lädt Eltern und Kinder gleichermaßen zum Aufenthalt ein. 

Das Bistro Affenstark wird von den Pächtern vegetarisch und vegan geführt. 

Rund ums Jahr gibt es Events für Groß und Klein, um Spaß zu haben und die Tiere näher kennenzulernen. Mehr Informationen dazu auf unserer Homepage. 

Der Leintalzoo hat 365 Tage im Jahr geöffnet. 
Sommerzeit: 10-18 Uhr 
Winterzeit: 10-18 Uhr 

Hunde, die an der Leine geführt werden, sind im Tierpark willkommen.

Name: Soper Creek Wildlife Rescue & Ranch
Description:
Soper Creek Wildlife Rescue is Ontario's premier wildlife rehabilitation and education center, dedicated to protecting the province's incredible native species and inspiring a love for nature in all who visit. Located on 15 acres of picturesque wilderness, we offer a sanctuary where injured, orphaned, and displaced wildlife can receive the care they need to return to their natural habitats.

But we’re more than just a rescue. We believe education is the key to conservation. Our hands-on, interactive programming reaches thousands of students, families, and community members every year. From school workshops and summer camps to corporate team-building events, our goal is to foster a connection with the environment and empower individuals to take action in protecting our planet.

As an organization, we pride ourselves on collaboration. Through partnerships with conservation authorities, local businesses, and volunteers, we’re building a community of wildlife champions who share our mission of preserving Ontario's natural beauty for future generations.

Whether you’re passionate about environmental education, wildlife care, or making a difference in your community, Soper Creek Wildlife Rescue offers a unique opportunity to connect, learn, and grow.

Join us as we protect Ontario’s wildlife, one life at a time.

Name: Jardins Haute Couture
Description:
Nichés au creux d’un vallon, les jardins de La Couture forment un délicat écrin de verdure et de senteurs autour d’une ravissante demeure, anciennement la maison et l' atelier de M. Chauvelin, tailleur de pierre. C'est dans ces ateliers que Max Ernst a sculpté ses statues en tuffeau.  Superficie : 5 000 m2.

Ouverts sur la vallée de l’Indre et de la Loire, couronnés par la forêt de Chinon, les jardins en terraces dévoilent un à un leurs secrets dans un dédale de pergolas et de massifs richement colorés. Planté exclusivement de plantes médicinales, mellifères et aromatiques, le jardin se veut sanctuaire de la biodiversité cherchant à promouvoir l’agriculture biologique afin de protéger des espèces en danger telles les abeilles.

Les jardins sont inscrits au collège des Parcs et des jardins de la région Centre. Ils sont ouverts à la visite de mai à octobre.

Name: Ngā Manu Nature Reserve Waikanae
Description:
Escape into the serenity of Ngā Manu Nature Reserve located on the Kāpiti Coast. Ngā Manu Nature Reserve is in Waikanae, on the Kāpiti Coast, 50 min drive north of Wellington. The reserve is a haven for native birds and wildlife and features a coastal forest walk. 
Book a Kiwi Night Encounter and sneak out the back to the private enclosure where you get close to Kiwi in its outdoor habitat without them noticing you are there. Or book a Native Icons tour, showcasing New Zealand's favourite iconic animals the kiwi, Tuatara and Tuna (eel).
Theo's Cottage is set inside Ngā Manu Nature Reserve where your accommodation includes entry into the reserve day or night, for the duration of your stay. See native birds and reptiles and take a torch to try the forest walk at night. 
Take a ranger tour and see our wildlife behind the scenes and meet the birds’ cheeky personalities and juvenile kiwi in the nocturnal house. Sneak out the back after dark on our famous Kiwi Night Encounter to see kiwis up close foraging in a natural enclosure.
Take a ranger tour and see our wildlife behind the scenes. Meet the native birds and reptiles and hear their conservation and breeding programmes. 
View our juvenile kiwi in their nocturnal house. Your admission ticket is valid for the entire day so you can explore the reserve at your own pace.
See the native birds, tuatara and other reptiles and learn about the conservation programme. Connect back to nature with our native forest walk.
Discover New Zealand’s unique prehistoric reptile - the tuatara. Meet the friendly kea, tui, fantail, kereru, and kaka. Over 56 different bird species have been spotted over the years coming and going around the wetlands, bush, and surroundings - every visit to Nga Manu is different.

Name: Coastal Georgia Botanical Gardens
Description:
Coastal Georgia Botanical Garden's (CGBG) mission is to provide education, public outreach, and applied research in horticulture and environmental sciences.

We strive to provide practical community classes, inspiring and dynamic school programs, and spectacular plant collections that educate visitors to the many roles that plants play in our lives.

The Garden is a top Savannah attraction that serves as a museum of plants, a tranquil escape, a living classroom, and an impressive, historic venue for special events for more than 100,000 annual visitors. Located off Hwy 17 near the I-95 corridor, our visitors come from near and far!

Our Judge Arthur Solomon Camellia Trail boasts one of the most diverse and unique Camellia collections in North America, with over 900 plants across 500 cultivars and 38 species.

The annual December Nights and Holiday Lights display is one of the most-anticipated holiday events in the greater Savannah area.

The history of Coastal Georgia Botanical Gardens at the Historic Bamboo Farm (CGBG) dates back to c. 1890, when the first clump of Japanese Timber Bamboo was planted on the 46-acre Smith family farm. Garnering interest from Dr. David Fairchild, the USDA purchased the bamboo groves and remaining property in 1919. The Bamboo Farm served as a USDA Plant Introduction and Research Station for 60 years before it was acquired by the University of Georgia.  Since the 1990s, the property has transitioned from a research station into a botanical garden and was officially renamed Coastal Georgia Botanical Gardens at the Historic Bamboo Farm in 2012. Though to many locals, it will always be affectionately known as "the Bamboo Farm". 

Today, the Coastal Georgia Botanical Gardens remains under the auspices of the University of Georgia’s College of Agricultural and Environmental Sciences and UGA Extension.

Name: Tata Steel Zoological Park
Description: Located in the heart of Jamshedpur, Tata Steel Zoological Park was established in 1990 and formally opened to the public on March 3, 1994. Operated by the Tata Steel Zoological Society, a charitable NGO supported by the Tata Steel Foundation, the zoo is recognized as a ‘Medium’ category zoo by the Central Zoo Authority (CZA) under the Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972. Spread across 72 acres—expanded from its earlier 57 acres—the zoo is scenically located on the banks of the Subarnarekha River, along Marine Drive near the southern boundary of the city. Historically, this land formed a part of the iconic Jubilee Park, which was inaugurated by Prime Minister Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru in 1956.

Description:
Great Ape Heart Project is based at the Detroit Zoological Society since 2021 and was founded at Zoo Atlanta in 2010.

We are a group of dedicated and coordinated subject matter experts that provide a network of clinical, pathologic, and research strategies to aid in the understanding and treating of cardiac disease in all the ape species, with the ultimate goal of reducing cardiovascular-related mortalities and improving the health and welfare of great apes in managed care.

Name: Abenteuer Affenberg
Description:
Affenberg Landskron, also known as Monkey Mountain Landskron, is a renowned wildlife park near Villach, Austria. The park is home to a large population of Japanese macaques, or snow monkeys, living in a semi-wild environment. Serving as both an educational institution and a popular tourist destination, it offers a unique blend of research and recreational activities for visitors from around the world. 

Functioning as a Field Research Station of the University of Vienna, Affenberg coordinates and oversees international research projects aimed at promoting animal welfare and advancing understanding of primate behavior. International students conduct cutting-edge research in a responsible and ethical manner. 

Guided tours offer visitors the opportunity to experience the monkeys up close as they freely roam their forested habitat. The park places a strong focus on educating visitors about the monkeys' behavior, habitat, and conservation efforts.

Name: Naturschutz-Tierpark Görlitz e.V.
Description:
Der Naturschutz-Tierpark Görlitz-Zgorzelec ist kein Zoo wie jeder andere. Mit seinen fünf Hektar Fläche gehört er zwar eher zu den kleineren Zoos Deutschlands, viel zu entdecken gibt es aber trotzdem! Etwa 500 Tiere in knapp 100 Arten werden hier in naturnahen und liebevoll gestalteten Gehegen präsentiert, stets mit besonderem Augenmerk auf die Qualität der Tierhaltung und des Artenschutzes.
Ein Herzensanliegen des Tierparks ist die Sensibilisierung unserer Gesellschaft für den Erhalt unserer Natur, einschließlich bedrohter Wildtierarten und alter Haustierrassen. Gelegenheiten zu tierischen Kontakten der besonderen Art lauern an jeder Ecke. Haben Sie z.B. schon mal ein Stachelschwein gefüttert? Einem Kamel tief in die Augen geschaut? Auf einer Bank inmitten von Felsenhörnchen und Chinasittichen dem Plätschern eines Wasserfalls gelauscht? Einen Fischotter nur durch eine Scheibe getrennt bei seiner Mahlzeit beobachtet? All das und noch viel mehr ist möglich im Naturschutz-Tierpark!

Name: Affenberg Salem
Description:
Affenberg Salem is the perfect place for a your trip to Lake Constance and is one of the most popular destinations and sights in the Lake Constance region. No matter whether you are planning a family trip with kids, an outdoor experience with your partner, friends or a group trip.

With us you will experience animals as if they were in the wild and under optimal keeping conditions. By the way, we specialize in keeping monkeys - Barbary macaques to be precise! Almost 200 monkeys roam freely in a 20-hectare forest. Therefore, we are not comparable to a zoo or conventional animal park, because what sets us apart from a zoo or animal park is that there are no barriers to separate the visitors. You find yourself in the middle of the animals. In addition, during the summer months more than 100 storks live on the Affenberg-rooftops. It is the largest free-flying stork colony in southern Germany.

Plan your visit: www.affenberg-salem.de/en

Name: Texas Zoofari Park
Description:
The Texas Zoofari Park, a 900-acre safari park, opened in March 2023 and works to provide animals with as natural a habitat as possible for the more than 600 animals living there. It features a 6-mile road where guests can feed Banteng, Grevy's and Grants Zebras, Watusi, Bison, Eland, Sable Antelope, and many other exotic animals free-roaming and up close, without barriers.

The park also features engaging walk-through exhibits where guests enjoy feeding reticulated giraffe along with our Adventure Budgie Aviary. The Texas Zoofari Park is currently in expansion mode with new exhibits and exciting behind-the-scenes encounters in development.

Name: LE JARDIN DES CINQ SENS - Yvoire
Description:
Au cœur du charmant village d’Yvoire, Le Jardin des Cinq Sens s'est inspiré de l’ambiance poétique des labyrinthes du Moyen-Age. Plusieurs jardins intimistes aux noms évocateurs invitent à l’éveil des sens et à la découverte : Jardin du Goût, Jardin de l’Odorat, Cloître de plantes médicinales, Prairie Alpine… Chaque univers est un hommage à la richesse du monde végétal où l’homme et la plante se retrouvent à merveille.
Créé il y a plus de 30 ans, ce jardin est une œuvre vivante patiemment ciselée où chacun entre à son rythme. Rosiers parfumés à la floraison prolongée, légumes oubliés et curiosités potagères, plantes médicinales ou plantes légendaires, feuillages aux textures étonnantes, collection de plantes odorantes... Pas moins de 1500 variétés évoluent au fil des saisons pour le plaisir de chacun. Une quête de diversité permanente menée par une équipe de jardiniers passionnés heureuse de partager son savoir-faire.

Name: Bear Country USA
Description: Nestled in over 200 acres amidst towering pines and along rolling meadows just eight miles south of Rapid City, Bear Country U.S.A. offers visitors intimate views of most North American mammals. Visitors take a leisurely three-mile drive through numerous enclosures and encounter black bear, elk, reindeer, arctic wolves, bighorn sheep, dall sheep, gray wolves, cougars, pronghorn, mule deer and buffalo. After the drive, you cannot miss out on our Wildlife Walkway area, which exhibits over 15 different species of small North American wildlife, including our playful black bear cubs!

in all its endeavours apply the principals of good governance and meet all compliance and regulatory requirements.

Name: Terrarium
Description:
Terrarium is a team of passionate terrarium makers who believe in the beauty of nature and its ability to positively impact our lives. We create and curate a wide range of stunning terrariums that are perfect for adding a touch of greenery and elegance to any space.

Our terrariums are carefully crafted using a variety of high-quality materials, including glass containers, soil, sand, rocks, and a variety of plants. We offer a range of styles and sizes to suit every taste and budget, from simple and minimalistic designs to more elaborate and intricate creations.

At Terrarium, we take pride in our commitment to sustainability and environmental responsibility. Name: eleCREW
Description: eleCREW has a vision of an Africa in which elephants and humans co-exist in a balanced environment.  We aim to achieve this through our CREW methodology, this stands for Community; Research; Education; & Welfare.  We care for 9 elephants, our ambassador herd, on a 3000 ha wildlife reserve in Victoria Falls, Zimbabwe.  The ongoing welfare of these 9 elephants is our primary objective and we actively work with partner organisations to monitor, evaluate and improve this.  Ensuring the best quality of life possible for the ambassador herd and utilising our humane  'Cooperative Teaching' methods we are in a unique position to impact our vision.  Our community programs target towns, villages and rural communities in the Matableland North province of Zimbabwe who live side by side with elephants, promoting coexistence.  The research programs give access to a unique situation to study all disciplines that involve understanding elephants and how we can improve their welfare and conservation. We also run a unique immersive education program called 'Through The Eyes Of An Elephant'.  This we subsidise school and community groups in our target population to benefit from.  It is designed to a) change perceptions to be more positive and acknowledge elephants as sentient beings; b) increase knowledge uptake about all aspect of elephants from mental capacities to physiology; & c) encourage uptake of humane methods to mitigate the negative impacts of living next to elephants.  This program is both informative and entertaining and is open to paying tourists who can have an up-close experience with the world's largest land mammal, understand the world through their eyes and know that their donations are going back to a genuine cuase to benefit the welfare and conservation of these iconic animals.

Name: Tierpark Sababurg

Name: Le Jardin Secret De Pan
Description:
Situé à Evreux en Normandie, le Jardin secret de Pan est un petit jardin privé de ville, un lieu envoûtant où les plantes vous invitent dans un voyage autant botanique qu'onirique.
Réalisé et entretenu par deux passionnés, il abrite une collection de plus de 500 plantes sur moins de 800 m². Ici, les plantes les plus simples s'harmonisent avec les plus complexes, les plus rustiques mettent en valeur les plus exotiques ! Bien souvent une même plante est déclinée en plusieurs variétés...
Ses propriétaires ont à coeur de partager cette passion avec vous, de vous faire découvrir tout ce petit monde pour une parenthèse hors du temps, un moment de dépaysement.

Peter ouvre les portes du jardin également tous les mercredis après-midi de 14h à 18h du 15 mai au 30 septembre 

Le jardin étant situé dans une impasse, il est conseillé de stationner en dehors de celle-ci. L'entrée du jardin est offerte.

Le jardin secret de Pan, 10 impasse du champ d'enfer 27000 EVREUX

Name: Chifwema Arboretum
Description:
Chifwema Arboretum seeks to be the “go to place” for all things related to trees in Zambia. Not only will it be a living museum of indigenous trees and plants, providing information on trees and their complex interactions with the environment, but also an education and training centre for school and university students and persons interested in making tree conservation their livelihood.

The purpose of the Chifwema Arboretum is to provide a permanent space of about 30 acres in perpetuity, to exhibit and celebrate indigenous trees and shrubs. It is a place where many different trees and shrubs are grown for research,  study of ethno-botany and related traditional medicine, aesthetic appreciation and botanically related conservation and education. It is our conviction that the Chifwema Arboretum will enhance knowledge and understanding of our indigenous trees and plants, their ecology, and the benefits they bring to the environment, our climate and to the wellbeing of humans and wildlife. The Chifwema Arboretum will also provide livelihood opportunities for those who seek to become involved in nature, the environment and its conservation.

Name: Jardins botaniques de France et des Pays Francophones
Description:

De multiplier les rapports qu'ils sont appelés à avoir entre eux pour l'extension et la promotion des Jardins botaniques, le perfectionnement de leurs connaissances, l'échange d'idées, d'expériences et de plantes de collection,

De participer à la conservation des plantes et des biotopes menacés, dans toutes les zones géographiques, d'intervenir en tant que consultant dans les projets d'aménagement mettant en jeu l'environnement,

D'organiser des séminaires, journées d'études, conférences ainsi que la diffusion des connaissances nouvelles, et favoriser leur rôle éducatif,

De prévoir et d'établir une participation ou coordination avec les pouvoirs publics et les autres associations similaires françaises ou étrangères,

De servir de conseil et de défense pour les statuts des personnels des Jardins botaniques,

D'aider à la création ou au développement d'autres Jardins botaniques à l'échelle internationale

Name: Betty Ford Alpine Gardens
Description:
Betty Ford Alpine Gardens in Vail, Colorado, is an internationally acclaimed botanic garden known for unique alpine plants, education programs and conservation practices. At 8200', it is North America's highest botanical garden with a mission to "to protect the alpine environment through education, conservation and living plant collections.". "

As the sole keeper of the nation’s exclusive collection of Colorado’s alpine flora, the Betty Ford Alpine Gardens is actively engaged in the conservation of alpine plants from around the world. Included in the Colorado alpine collection are rare and endangered species endemic to the state, including the Parachute penstemon, an unusual plant that grows only in oil shale talus along steep cliffs in western Colorado, and the Hoosier Pass ipomopsis, a member of the phlox family that is found only above 10,000 feet in the Mosquito Range." -Sarah Chase Shaw

While a stunning place to visit there is much more to this Garden. Each year a new education exhibit will surprise even the most knowledgeable visitor and visitors can learn about the scientific work being accomplished in the alpine to save the fragile species that call it home.

The outdoor gardens are open dawn until dusk year-round, with snow on the ground November through April. The Education Center provides exhibits, programs and features an Alpine House. See the Gardens book "On the Roof of the Rocky Mountains: The Botanical Legacy of Betty Ford Alpine Gardens".

Name: Sherman Library & Gardens
Description: The Sherman Library & Gardens is a non-profit that has been deeply rooted in Orange County for over half of a century, serving the community as a sanctuary and education beacon for history, horticulture and the arts. Founded in 1966 by Arnold Haskell, and named for his friend and mentor, Moses Hazeltine Sherman, this iconic institution serves as a guardian of regional history and artifacts, a living library of plants both native and exotic, and a conservator of artistic works influenced by and produced in the Pacific Southwest.
